close_reason: Added sign_verify_with_local_signing_backend test using ed25519-dalek Verifier as oracle
description: The sign_parses_64_byte_signature test for Ed25519 (lib.rs:249-254) feeds [42u8; 64] to a mock backend and asserts the signature bytes round-trip as [42u8; 64]. The RSA sign_parses_signature test (lib.rs:331-337) checks only that to_bytes().len() == 256. Neither test uses an independent oracle. The Ed25519 test only verifies that ed25519::Signature::from_slice accepts any 64 bytes — it does not confirm the adapter is wired correctly to a real key. A backend that returns zeroed bytes of the right length passes all tests. Use known test vectors (e.g. RFC 8032 Ed25519 vectors, NIST ECDSA vectors) against a real or reference signing implementation, not mock bytes.
title: Tests use mock as oracle, not independent test vectors
